title: Render refs/bookmarks with platform-style icons instead of colored text
description:
Replace the current colored text rendering of refs/bookmarks with fancy icons matching the platform's default Commit column appearance.

## Current Implementation

Custom Description column renders refs as colored text:
- Bold, smaller font
- Color from `VcsRef.type.backgroundColor`
- Functional but less polished than platform's version

Example: `description text  bookmark1 tag1 @`

## Platform's Approach

Default Commit column uses sophisticated icon-based rendering:
- **Bookmarks**: Small golden bookmark icon (path-based shape)
- **Tags**: Colored rounded rectangles with tag name
- **References**: Colored labels with ref type colors
- **Compact mode**: Icons only, no text
- **Tooltips**: Hover shows full ref details

## Why This Is Hard

All icon rendering infrastructure is **internal-only**:

### 1. VcsLogLabelPainter (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/VcsLogLabelPainter.kt`:
```kotlin
internal class VcsLogLabelPainter(
    private val logData: VcsLogData, 
    component: JComponent, 
    iconCache: LabelIconCache
) : LabelPainter(component, iconCache) {
    fun customizePainter(
        references: Collection<VcsRef>,
        bookmarks: Collection<VcsBookmarkRef>,
        // ...
    )
}
```

### 2. BookmarkIcon (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/BookmarkIcon.java`:
```java
public class BookmarkIcon implements Icon {
  private final Color BOOKMARK_REF_TYPE_COLOR = 
      JBColor.namedColor("VersionControl.RefLabel.bookmarkBackground",
                         new JBColor(0xf4af3d, 0xd9a343));
  
  @Override
  public void paintIcon(Component c, Graphics g, int iconX, int iconY) {
    // Draws bookmark path shape
    Path2D.Float path = new Path2D.Float();
    path.moveTo(x, y);
    // ... bookmark shape
    g2.fill(path);
  }
}
```

### 3. LabelPainter (internal)
From `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelPainter.java`:
- Manages icon positioning and sizing
- Handles compact vs. expanded mode
- Calculates available width and truncation
- All methods are `internal` or package-private

### 4. LabelIcon (internal)
Renders colored rounded rectangles for ref labels.

## Possible Approaches (without custom log pane)

### 1. Copy/Reimplement Icons (high effort)
- Copy `BookmarkIcon`, `LabelIcon` source code
- Reimplement `LabelPainter` logic
- Maintain as platform updates
- **Risk**: Legal/licensing concerns, high maintenance

### 2. Reflection Access (fragile)
```kotlin
// Try to instantiate internal classes via reflection
val painterClass = Class.forName("com.intellij.vcs.log.ui.render.VcsLogLabelPainter")
// Very fragile, breaks with obfuscation/changes
```

### 3. Custom Icons (medium effort)
- Create our own bookmark/tag icon implementations
- Won't match platform exactly but close approximation
- Reasonable approach if custom log pane not pursued

## Proper Solution

**Requires custom VCS log pane** (see epic jj-idea-gyo):
- Implement custom renderer with access to internal classes
- Can use reflection or direct instantiation in custom UI
- Full control over rendering pipeline
- Can match platform appearance exactly

## Visual Comparison

**Current (colored text)**:
```
Fix bug in parser  main trunk @
```

**Desired (with icons)**:
```
Fix bug in parser  [📌 main] [📦 trunk] [@]
```
(where [📌] is golden bookmark icon, [📦] is colored rounded label, etc.)

## Investigation References

Platform files studied:
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/VcsLogLabelPainter.kt`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/BookmarkIcon.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelPainter.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/LabelIcon.java`
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/GraphCommitCellRenderer.kt`

## Dependency
Blocked by jj-idea-gyo (Custom VCS log pane implementation)

## Priority
P2 - Nice to have, current colored text is functional

title: Replace/hide default Commit (description) column
description:
Remove or hide the platform's default "Commit" column so only the custom Jujutsu Description column is visible, eliminating redundancy.

## Current Situation

Both columns are currently visible:
1. **Default "Commit" column** (platform):
   - Shows commit subject/description
   - Has fancy bookmark/tag icons
   - Cannot be hidden (not a dynamic column)
   - Always appears before custom columns

2. **Custom "Jujutsu.Description" column**:
   - Shows description with grey/italic for empty
   - Shows refs/bookmarks with colored text
   - Has tooltip with full description
   - Can be hidden by user

## Problem

- Duplicate information displayed
- User must manually hide custom column or live with redundancy
- Default column cannot be hidden via UI or API
- Default column always appears first, custom column comes after

## Why This Is Hard

The default "Commit" column is **hardcoded as non-dynamic**:

From `VcsLogDefaultColumn.kt`:
```kotlin
object Commit : VcsLogDefaultColumn<GraphCommitCell>(
    "Default.Subject", 
    VcsLogBundle.message("vcs.log.column.subject"), 
    false  // <-- isDynamic = false, cannot be hidden
)

companion object {
  // Root and Commit are always included
  private val defaultColumns = listOf(Root, Commit, Author, Date, Hash)
}
```

From `VcsLogColumnUtil.kt`:
```kotlin
fun getDynamicColumns(): List<VcsLogColumn<*>> {
  val columns: MutableList<VcsLogColumn<*>> = ArrayList()
  columns.addAll(getDefaultDynamicColumns())  // Only Author, Hash, Date
  columns.addAll(VcsLogCustomColumn.KEY.extensionList)
  return columns.filter { it.isDynamic }  // Commit filtered out here
}
```

**No public API exists** to:
- Change `isDynamic` property of default columns
- Remove columns from the default column list
- Override column manager behavior
- Programmatically hide specific columns

## Possible Workarounds (without custom log pane)

### 1. Reflection Hack (fragile)
```kotlin
// Access internal VcsLogColumnManager and remove Commit column
// Very fragile, breaks with platform updates
```

### 2. Override Default Column (might not work)
```kotlin
// Try registering a column with id "Default.Subject"
// May conflict with platform's registration
```

### 3. Accept Redundancy (current approach)
- Document that users should manually hide one column
- Not ideal UX but functional

## Proper Solution

**Requires custom VCS log pane** (see epic jj-idea-gyo):
- Implement custom `VcsLogUi` that excludes default Commit column
- Full control over column list and behavior
- Significant implementation effort

## Investigation References

Platform files studied:
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogDefaultColumn.kt` (lines 37, 83-145)
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogColumnUtil.kt` (lines 45-127)
- `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/table/column/VcsLogColumnManager.kt` (lines 30-56)

## Dependency
Blocked by jj-idea-gyo (Custom VCS log pane implementation)

## Priority
P2 - Annoying but not blocking, users can manually hide the unwanted column

title: [Epic] Custom VCS log pane implementation
description:
# Custom VCS Log Pane Implementation

Replace IntelliJ's default VCS log UI with a custom implementation to enable deep customization of the log view that's not possible with the current public API.

## Why Custom Implementation Needed

The IntelliJ VCS log framework has several limitations for plugins:

1. **Fixed default columns**: `Root` and `Commit` columns are hardcoded and cannot be hidden or reordered
2. **No column visibility control**: No public API to programmatically set column visibility (all column management APIs are `internal`)
3. **Limited rendering customization**: Cannot use platform's internal rendering infrastructure (`VcsLogLabelPainter`, `BookmarkIcon`, etc.)
4. **Column ordering restrictions**: Custom columns always appear after fixed platform columns

## Investigation Findings

### API Research (2025-12-21)

**VCS Log Tab Creation APIs**:
- `VcsLogContentUtil.openLogTab()` - Exists but requires already-created VcsLogUi
- `VcsLogManager.getInstance()`, `createLogUi()` - Not available in public SDK
- `VcsLogManager.openNewLogTab()` - Used internally by platform, not exposed
- Most tab creation methods are in internal classes not accessible to plugins

**Working Prototype Created**:
- Service: `JujutsuCustomLogTabManager`
- Action: `OpenJujutsuLogTabAction`
- Currently opens standard VCS log (uses our custom columns via extension points)
- Files: `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/JujutsuCustomLogTabManager.kt`

**Limitations with Standard Approach**:
- ✅ Custom columns work (Status, Change ID, Description, Committer)
- ❌ Default columns (Root, Commit) cannot be hidden
- ❌ Cannot control column ordering
- ❌ Cannot access internal rendering APIs

### Implementation Approach - Path B (Full Custom)

**Decision**: Proceed with full custom VcsLogUi implementation despite complexity

**Strategy**:
1. Study IntelliJ platform source for VcsLogUi patterns
2. Create custom VcsLogUiFactory implementation
3. Build custom table model and column manager
4. Use reflection or code copying to access internal rendering APIs
5. Manage lifecycle properly to avoid memory leaks

**Key Platform Classes to Study**:
- `VcsLogUiImpl` - Main log UI implementation
- `VcsLogGraphTable` - Table component
- `GraphTableModel` - Table data model
- `VcsLogColumnManager` - Column management (internal)
- `VcsLogLabelPainter`, `BookmarkIcon` - Rendering (internal)

## What Custom Implementation Would Enable

1. **Complete column control**:
   - Hide or replace default Commit column
   - Position custom columns first
   - Programmatic column visibility defaults

2. **Advanced rendering**:
   - Use platform's label painters and icons
   - Custom graph rendering
   - Right-aligned refs in description column

3. **Custom UI/UX**:
   - Different table layout
   - Custom row rendering
   - Additional UI controls

## Dependent Tasks

Tasks that require custom log pane:
- jj-idea-dtc: Render refs/bookmarks with platform-style icons
- jj-idea-n0o: Right-align references in Description column
- jj-idea-u4g: Replace/hide default Commit column

## Complexity Assessment

**Very High** - This is a significant undertaking:
- Need to understand and replicate substantial platform VCS log infrastructure
- Requires reflection or code copying to access internal APIs
- Maintenance burden as platform evolves
- Risk of breaking with platform updates

**Mitigation**:
- Start with basic feature parity
- Isolate internal API usage for easier updates
- Comprehensive testing
- Document all assumptions and workarounds

## Priority

P2 - High priority. Extension point approach cannot achieve required customizations.

title: Right-align references/bookmarks in Description column
description:
Currently, references (bookmarks, tags, @) are appended inline after the description text. For better visual alignment with the platform's default Commit column, these should be right-aligned within the column.

## Implementation Challenge

This requires custom painting beyond what `ColoredTableCellRenderer` provides:

1. **ColoredTableCellRenderer limitation**: Only supports left-aligned text appending
2. **Solution requires**: Override `paintComponent()` to manually position refs on the right
3. **Similar to platform approach**: Platform uses `VcsLogLabelPainter` with explicit x-coordinate positioning

## Technical Details

Platform's approach (from investigation):
- `GraphCommitCellRenderer.RealCommitRendererComponent.paintComponent()` calculates position:
  ```kotlin
  val start = max(graphWidth, (width - referencePainter.getSize().width)).toInt()
  referencePainter.paint(g2d, start, 0, height)
  ```
- Uses `LabelPainter` (internal API) which measures ref widths and paints at specific coordinates

## Custom Implementation Needed

Would require:
1. Custom `TableCellRenderer` extending `JPanel` or similar
2. Override `paintComponent()` to paint description left, refs right
3. Measure available width and ref widths to calculate positioning
4. Handle truncation when description + refs exceed column width

## Related Files
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/log/JujutsuVcsLogColumns.kt` (JujutsuDescriptionRenderer)
- Platform reference: `platform/vcs-log/impl/src/com/intellij/vcs/log/ui/render/GraphCommitCellRenderer.kt`

## Dependency
May be easier to implement as part of custom VCS log pane implementation (see beads-XXX).

## Priority
P2 - Nice to have, current inline display is functional

title: Implement RepositoryLocation for remote repository integration
description:
Implement RepositoryLocation for integration with remote repository (GitHub, GitLab, etc).

Currently getChangedRepositoryPath() returns null. Should return proper RepositoryLocation for:
- Linking to remote repository in history view
- "Open in GitHub/GitLab" actions
- Remote diff viewing

**Location**: JujutsuFileRevision.kt line 44

**Implementation**:
- Determine if repo has remote configured
- Map file path to remote repository URL
- Return RepositoryLocation with remote URL

**Related**: jj-idea-4kg.8 (Open in remote)

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Returns RepositoryLocation when remote is configured
- [ ] Null for local-only repositories
- [ ] Works with GitHub, GitLab, etc.
close_reason: Reverted and closing: RepositoryLocation is not needed. The 'Open in remote' feature (jj-idea-4kg.8) does not use RepositoryLocation at all — IntelliJ's Open on GitHub works through Git hosting service infrastructure (GitRepositoryManager, HostedGitRepositoriesManager), not through VcsFileRevision.getChangedRepositoryPath(). The only platform features that use getChangedRepositoryPath() are ShowAllAffectedGenericAction and CreatePatchFromDirectoryAction, both of which also require CommittedChangesProvider which we don't implement. This was premature abstraction with no consumer.

title: Localise all resources
Bookmark management UI
description:
**Priority**: HIGH (Phase 2 - Simple write operations)
**Effort**: 2-3 days

## Description
Create/delete/rename/move bookmarks (Jujutsu's version of branches).

## User Experience
- Bookmarks shown in log view
- Right-click change → "Create Bookmark Here"
- Right-click bookmark → "Delete Bookmark"
- Right-click bookmark → "Rename Bookmark"
- Right-click bookmark → "Move to Change..."

## Implementation

design:
## Design Summary

### Scope
Core bookmark CRUD via log context menu and VCS menu/toolbar. Follow-ups for bookmark widget and interactive decorations tracked separately.

### Design Decisions
- **Create** uses `jj bookmark create` (fails if exists - clear error, not silently moving)
- **Move** uses `jj bookmark set -r <revision>` (create-or-update expected for move intent)
- **Multi-bookmark**: Submenu listing per-bookmark delete/rename actions
- **Confirmation**: Only delete prompts; create/rename/move are easily undoable

### Implementation

**CommandExecutor** (4 new methods):
- bookmarkCreate(name, revision)
- bookmarkDelete(name)
- bookmarkRename(oldName, newName)
- bookmarkSet(name, revision)

**CliExecutor**: Top-level testable arg builders (bookmarkCreateArgs, etc.) + override implementations.

**Action factories** (4 new files in vcs/actions/):
- bookmarkCreateAction(project, entry?) - prompts for name, creates at entry.id
- bookmarkDeleteAction(project, repo?, bookmark?) - confirms, deletes
- bookmarkRenameAction(project, repo?, bookmark?) - prompts new name
- bookmarkMoveAction(project, entry?) - loads bookmarks off EDT, popup chooser, sets

**Registered action**: BookmarkCreateAction class for VCS menu + toolbar (follows GitFetchAction pattern).

**Log context menu** (between rebase/squash and fetch/push):
- "Create Bookmark Here..." (always, single selection)
- "Bookmark >" submenu (if entry has bookmarks): per-bookmark Delete/Rename
- "Move Bookmark Here..." (always, single selection)

**Toolbar**: Add Jujutsu.BookmarkCreate to CommitTablePanel.createActionGroup() after fetch/push.

**Input helper**: requestBookmarkName() in Inputs.kt (single-line dialog).

**Bundle properties**: ~20 message keys for actions, errors, dialogs.

**Tests**: CliExecutorBookmarkTest following CliExecutorGitRemoteTest pattern.

### Files
New (6): bookmarkCreateAction.kt, bookmarkDeleteAction.kt, bookmarkRenameAction.kt, bookmarkMoveAction.kt, BookmarkCreateAction.kt, CliExecutorBookmarkTest.kt
Modified (7): CommandExecutor.kt, CliExecutor.kt, Inputs.kt, JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt, CommitTablePanel.kt, plugin.xml, JujutsuBundle.properties

Split out command executor by domain
description: Today, we have a giant command executor responsible for every JJ function. While this was helpful to get started, it has now become unwieldy and keeps growing as we add more functionality. It is time to split this out into different domains. Perhaps we could have an executor for each high-level concern, sometimes identified by the first parameter passed to the JJ command line (e.g. git, bookmark, op) but other times with similar operations grouped (e.g. split, squash, rebase; log, status, show; etc.)

title: Make command results domain-specific
description:
Today, commands all return command results, which contain an exit code, stdout and stderr. This does not abstract away the transport mechanism: command results are specific to CLI execution. Worse, the openness of these results means that client code is responsible for interpreting the results, including exit code, to figure out what happened, pushing this responsibility to the wrong side of the command boundary. This will make it difficult to split out execution to have different back ends, which was one of the original points of having a separate 'command executor' interface.

Better would be to ensure that commands in the executor have domain-specific responses, with all of the parsing within the executor implementation.

title: Add refresh suppression for compound VCS operations
description:
When a VCS action chains multiple jj commands (e.g. squash + edit @-), file changes from intermediate commands trigger the BulkFileListener's debounced refresh, causing flickering and potentially stale intermediate states in the UI.

## Problem
The BulkFileListener in JujutsuStateModel debounces file changes at 300ms and fires repositoryStates.invalidate(). When two jj commands run back-to-back (e.g. squash then edit), the first command's file changes may trigger a refresh before the second command completes, showing a brief intermediate state.

## Proposed solution
Add a suppression/batching mechanism to JujutsuStateModel:

```kotlin
fun <T> JujutsuRepository.withRefreshSuppressed(action: () -> T): T {
    stateModel.suppressRefresh()
    try {
        return action()
    } finally {
        stateModel.resumeRefresh()  // fires single invalidation if any were suppressed
    }
}
```

The BulkFileListener and explicit invalidate() calls check the suppression flag. If suppressed, they set a 'dirty' flag instead of firing. When suppression ends, a single invalidation fires if anything was suppressed.

## Affected operations
- Squash into parent (squash + edit @-)
- Future compound operations

## Integration with jj-idea-i4k5
When logRefresh notifier is added, suppression should cover both repositoryStates and logRefresh. The suppression mechanism should be generic enough to work with both NotifiableState and Notifier.

## Related
- jj-idea-i4k5: logRefresh notifier (suppression should integrate with this)
- jj-idea-4tbs: UI updates frequently (suppression reduces unnecessary refreshes)

title: UI updates frequently
description:
The log reloads on every file change in the IDE because it subscribes to `repositoryStates`, which invalidates via BulkFileListener on a 300ms debounce. File edits don't change log history — only the working copy entry's empty/non-empty flag could change, and that only matters to the working copy panel.

After jj-idea-i4k5 decouples the log from `repositoryStates`, decide whether `logRefresh` should fire on file changes at all, or only on explicit VCS operations.
design:
## Problem

The log reloads on every file change because it subscribes to `repositoryStates`, which invalidates on a 300ms debounce from BulkFileListener. Editing a file in the IDE triggers a full log reload even though only the working copy entry could have changed.

## Analysis

After jj-idea-i4k5 is implemented, the log subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. This creates two options:

1. **Don't fire `logRefresh` from BulkFileListener at all** — file edits don't change log history, only the WC entry. The WC panel already handles this via `repositoryStates`. The log would only refresh on explicit VCS operations and the refresh button.

2. **Debounce `logRefresh` separately** with a longer interval (e.g., 1-2 seconds) for file-triggered refreshes vs immediate for VCS operations.

Option 1 is simpler and probably correct — the log shows commit history, not live file status. The WC entry in the log (empty/non-empty flag, description) only changes via VCS operations, not file edits.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 must land first to decouple log refresh from `repositoryStates`.

notes:
## Analysis (2026-02-08)

The commit details panel file change context menu uses **custom Jujutsu-specific actions** rather than **standard platform actions**.

### Current implementation (`fileChangeActionGroup()`):
- `Jujutsu.ShowChangesDiff`
- `Jujutsu.CompareWithLocal`
- `Jujutsu.CompareBeforeWithLocal`
- `Jujutsu.OpenChangeFile`
- `Jujutsu.OpenRepositoryVersion`
- `Jujutsu.RestoreFile` / `Jujutsu.RestoreToChange`

### Standard platform approach (`Vcs.RepositoryChangesBrowserMenu`):
- `Vcs.ShowDiffWithLocal`
- `Vcs.ShowDiffWithLocal.Before`
- `Vcs.EditSource`
- `Vcs.OpenRepositoryVersion`
- `Vcs.RevertSelectedChanges`
- `Vcs.GetVersion`
- `Vcs.ShowHistoryForRevision`

### How platform actions work

Platform actions like `Vcs.ShowDiffWithLocal` use an **extension provider pattern** (`AnActionExtensionProvider`). Each context registers a provider that:
1. Checks `isActive()` for relevant data keys (e.g., `VcsLogDataKeys.VCS_LOG_COMMIT_SELECTION`)
2. Implements `update()` and `actionPerformed()` for that context

This gives consistent shortcuts, icons, names, and localization across all VCS plugins.

### To fix

1. Register `AnActionExtensionProvider` implementations for standard actions that work with Jujutsu's data context
2. Ensure `JujutsuChangesTree.uiDataSnapshot()` provides the data keys the providers expect
3. Use standard action group `Vcs.RepositoryChangesBrowserMenu` instead of custom `fileChangeActionGroup()`

### References
- `VcsLogChangesBrowser.kt` - uses `Vcs.Log.ChangesBrowser.Popup` which references `Vcs.RepositoryChangesBrowserMenu`
- `VcsActions.xml` - defines `Vcs.RepositoryChangesBrowserMenu` group
- `ShowDiffAfterWithLocalFromLogActionProvider.java` - example extension provider

design:
## Context

This bead is about the standard `VcsLogProvider.subscribeToRootRefreshEvents()` hook, which is for the built-in IntelliJ VCS log. Since we use a custom log panel (not IntelliJ's built-in log), this hook is less relevant — it would only matter if we keep the legacy log integration (jj-idea-v1o).

For the custom log, external JJ operations (CLI commands outside the IDE) should trigger a refresh. After jj-idea-i4k5, the `logRefresh` notifier is the right hook point.

## Design

**For external JJ operation detection**: Watch `.jj/repo/op_heads/` directory for changes. When op_heads change, an external operation has occurred. Fire `logRefresh.notify(Unit)` (debounced).

The BulkFileListener in `JujutsuStateModel` currently filters OUT `.jj` directory events via `isUnderJjDirectory()`. To detect external operations, add a separate check for `.jj/repo/op_heads/` changes:

```kotlin
// In BulkFileListener.after()
if (events.any { isOpHeadsChange(it) }) {
    // External JJ operation detected — debounce and refresh log
    logRefreshAlarm.cancelAllRequests()
    logRefreshAlarm.addRequest({ logRefresh.notify(Unit) }, 500)
}
```

**For the legacy VCS log hook** (`subscribeToRootRefreshEvents`): Same approach — watch op_heads and call `refresher.refresh(root)`. But this is only needed if the legacy log is kept.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 provides the `logRefresh` notifier to fire.

design:
## Context

`repositoryStates` recomputes all working copies across all repos when any single repo changes. After jj-idea-i4k5, this concern is orthogonal to the log refresh path — `repositoryStates` only serves the working copy panel and dirty scope.

## Design (unchanged from original)

Key `repositoryStates` by repository so that invalidation is per-repo. Options:

1. Change to `NotifiableState<Map<JujutsuRepository, LogEntry>>` with per-repo invalidation
2. Keep current structure but only reload the changed repo's entry, merging with cached entries for other repos

Option 2 is simpler: the loader receives a hint about which repo changed, loads only that repo's `jj log -r @`, and merges with the cached set.

## Relationship to jj-idea-i4k5

Independent. `logRefresh` fires unconditionally (no per-repo optimization needed for the log signal). `repositoryStates` per-repo optimization only affects the WC panel and dirty scope paths.

title: Handle divergent changes (branch conflicts) in VCS log
description:
When JJ has divergent changes (same change ID pointing to multiple commits), they produce the same hash. This causes issues with IntelliJ's VCS log cache.

## Current Workarounds

These fixes were added to address the assertion error:
```
Assertion failed: X details to store, yet Y indexes in cache
```

**Root cause**: IntelliJ's `TopCommitsCache.storeDetails()` uses `containsValue()` to filter duplicates, which relies on object equality. Without `equals`/`hashCode`, new metadata objects for the same commit were treated as different on refresh, causing the cache to have fewer entries than the result list.

**Fixes applied**:
1. Added `equals`/`hashCode` to `JujutsuCommitMetadataBase` and `JujutsuTimedCommit` based on change ID
2. Added deduplication by hash in `readFirstBlock` and `readAllHashes`

## Future Work

Currently we deduplicate by hash, which means only one divergent change is shown. Need to properly handle divergent changes to show all of them.

## Related Code
- jj/JujutsuCommitMetadataBase.kt - equals/hashCode implementation
- vcs/JujutsuTimedCommit.kt - equals/hashCode implementation
- vcs/JujutsuLogProvider.kt - deduplication logic

See: `jj log -r 'divergent_change_id'` to view divergent commits

title: File history: Open in remote (GitLab/GitHub)
design:
## Design: Open in Remote (GitHub/GitLab)

### How IntelliJ's Git Plugin Does It

The Git plugin's \"Open on GitHub\" does NOT use RepositoryLocation or VcsFileRevision.getChangedRepositoryPath(). Instead it uses a dedicated infrastructure:

- `HostedGitRepositoriesManager` discovers remotes reactively via Kotlin flows
- `GlobalHostedGitRepositoryReferenceActionGroup` provides context-aware actions
- GitHub plugin implements these interfaces and registers via `Git.Hosting.Open.In.Browser.Group` action group
- Data sources: VCS_FILE_REVISION (file history), VCS_LOG_COMMIT_SELECTION (log), VIRTUAL_FILE+EDITOR (editor)

We can't reuse this because it requires GitRepository objects from GitRepositoryManager, which won't exist for jj-registered roots.

### Proposed Approach: Simple Custom Action

Since we're a standalone VCS plugin (not a Git hosting plugin), implement a simpler self-contained approach:

**1. URL Builder** — Pure function that constructs web URLs:
```kotlin
object RemoteUrlBuilder {
    fun commitUrl(remoteUrl: String, commitHash: String): String?
    fun fileUrl(remoteUrl: String, commitHash: String, filePath: String, lineRange: IntRange? = null): String?
}
```

Supports GitHub and GitLab URL patterns by parsing the remote URL:
- SSH: `git@github.com:user/repo.git` → `https://github.com/user/repo`
- HTTPS: `https://github.com/user/repo.git` → `https://github.com/user/repo`
- Commit: `<base>/commit/<hash>`
- File: `<base>/blob/<hash>/<path>` (GitHub) or `<base>/-/blob/<hash>/<path>` (GitLab)

**2. Remote Resolution** — Use existing `CommandExecutor.gitRemoteList()`:
- Parse `<name> <url>` lines
- Prefer `origin` remote
- Cache per repository (remotes rarely change)

**3. Action Integration** — Add to existing context menus:

a) **File history** (JujutsuHistoryProvider): Add \"Open on Remote\" to `getAdditionalActions()`. Get commit hash from `JujutsuRevisionNumber` → need to expose `CommitId` from `JujutsuFileRevision` or store it in `JujutsuRevisionNumber`.

b) **Custom log** (JujutsuLogContextMenuActions): Add action that reads selected `LogEntry.commitId`.

c) **Editor/file** (optional, future): Open current file at working copy's commit on remote.

**4. Data Available Today**:
- `LogEntry.commitId: CommitId` — the git commit hash (full + short), already parsed
- `JujutsuRepository.commandExecutor.gitRemoteList()` — already implemented
- `JujutsuRevisionNumber` wraps a `Revision` (ChangeId), but NOT CommitId — need to expose CommitId somehow

### Key Decision: How to Get CommitId in File History

`JujutsuFileRevision` has access to `LogEntry` (which has `commitId`), but the platform action only sees `VcsFileRevision`. Options:
1. Add a `commitId` property to `JujutsuFileRevision` (simplest — cast in action)
2. Store both ChangeId and CommitId in `JujutsuRevisionNumber`
3. Pass LogEntry reference through

Option 1 is simplest and follows the pattern Git uses (casting to GitFileRevision).

### Scope

- GitHub and GitLab support (covers most jj users)
- Commit view only initially (file view is nice-to-have)
- Opens in default browser via `BrowserUtil.browse()`
- Graceful handling: disabled when no recognized remote, no error dialogs

### Test Plan

- Unit test `RemoteUrlBuilder` with GitHub SSH, GitHub HTTPS, GitLab SSH, GitLab HTTPS, unknown hosts
- Unit test remote list parsing
- Manual: runIde with a repo that has GitHub/GitLab remotes, verify URLs open correctly from log and file history

title: Set up UI integration tests for tree expansion behavior
description:
Set up full UI integration tests for the changes tree expansion behavior.

Current state:
- Unit tests exist for change detection logic (JujutsuTreeExpansionTest)
- Full UI tests need IntelliJ Platform test fixtures

What needs to be done:
1. Enable the full `test` task with proper IntelliJ test fixtures
2. Configure testFramework(TestFrameworkType.Platform) in build.gradle.kts
3. Create integration tests that:
   - Create mock projects with VCS changes
   - Test actual tree expansion/collapse behavior
   - Verify that new files expand parent directories
   - Verify that deleted files don't affect expansion state
   - Test mouse click handling (single click preview, double click diff)
   - Test keyboard shortcuts (Enter, F4)

Benefits:
- Catch UI regressions automatically
- Verify interaction with IntelliJ Platform APIs
- Test real-world scenarios with actual tree components

Related:
- Issue jj-idea-nk3: Run fuller test suite in CI (dependencies needed)
- JujutsuTreeExpansionTest.kt: Existing unit tests for logic

title: Remove or replace 'Not in any branch' message in log view details
description:
**Problem:**
The log view details pane (right-hand side) shows "Not in any branch" for all commits. This is incorrect for Jujutsu, which uses bookmarks instead of branches, and the Git concept of "current branch" doesn't apply.

**Current Behavior:**
- Details pane always shows: "Not in any branch"
- Message is misleading since JJ doesn't use Git-style branches

**Expected Behavior:**
Option 1 (Simple): Remove this field entirely
Option 2 (Better): Show bookmark information if the commit has bookmarks:
  - "Bookmarks: main, feature-x" (if commit has bookmarks)
  - Remove the field entirely if no bookmarks (don't show "no bookmarks")

Option 3 (Best): Show working copy indicator:
  - "@ Working Copy" (if this is the working copy commit)
  - "Bookmarks: main, feature-x" (if commit has bookmarks)
  - Remove field if neither applies

**Implementation Notes:**
- JujutsuLogProvider likely provides this through getCurrentBranch() which returns null
- May need to customize the log details panel rendering
- Could provide bookmark info through VcsLogProvider methods

**Related:**
- Issue #30: Display bookmarks as tags in log view
- JujutsuLogEntry has isWorkingCopy property that could be used

**Priority:** MEDIUM - Cosmetic issue but misleading to users
notes:
Requires same custom VCS log UI as jj-idea-rn8.

The 'Not in any branch' message comes from the standard CommitDetailsPanel which cannot be customized independently. Solution requires implementing custom VCS log UI via CustomVcsLogUiFactoryProvider.

Should be implemented together with jj-idea-rn8 since both need custom details pane.

Priority: Lower until custom VCS log UI foundation is built.

title: Show formatted change IDs in log view details pane
description:
**Problem:**
The log view details pane (right-hand side) currently shows the commit ID but not the change ID. When viewing a commit, users need to see the change ID prominently since that's the primary identifier in Jujutsu.

**Current Behavior:**
- Right-hand pane shows "Commit: <hash>" 
- No change ID visible
- No formatting (bold/color) applied to identifiers

**Expected Behavior:**
- Show change ID prominently: "Change: qpvuntsm" with formatting
  - Short prefix ("qp") in bold with highlight color
  - Remainder ("vuntsm") in muted color
- Optionally also show full commit ID below
- Use the same formatting as elsewhere in the UI (JujutsuCommitFormatter)

**Implementation Notes:**
- Likely need to customize the log view details pane rendering
- Apply JujutsuCommitFormatter.format() and toHtml() for consistent styling
- May need to extend VcsLogProvider or customize the details panel

**Priority:** HIGH - Change IDs are the primary way users interact with Jujutsu
notes:
Requires custom VCS log UI implementation.

Finding: Details pane cannot be customized independently. Must implement:
1. CustomVcsLogUiFactoryProvider extension
2. Custom VcsLogUi extending VcsLogUiImpl  
3. Override createMainFrame() to provide custom CommitDetailsPanel
4. Custom panel to display JJ change IDs with formatting

Example: Git plugin's BranchesInGitLogUiFactoryProvider

This is significant work (multiple components). Consider lower priority until other features complete.

```kotlin
class JujutsuMergeProvider(private val vcs: JujutsuVcs) : MergeProvider {

        // Parse conflict markers
        // Return base, ours, theirs
    }
}
```

## Conflict Detection
```kotlin
fun isConflicted(file: VirtualFile): Boolean {
    return content.contains("<<<<<<<") &&
           content.contains("=======") &&
           content.contains(">>>>>>>")
}
```

## Registration


title: New change command with options
description:
**Priority**: HIGH (Phase 2 - Simple write operations)
**Effort**: 1-2 days

## Description
Enhance "New Change" button to support creating change with description and options.

## Current Status
- ✅ "New" button exists
- ⚠️ Creates empty change, no description option

## User Experience
- Dialog: "Create new change"
- Optional description field
- Option: Create after current change vs. after parent
- Success notification with new change ID

## Implementation
```kotlin
fun createNewChange(description: String?, after: String?) {
    val args = mutableListOf("new")
    if (description != null) args.addAll(listOf("-m", description))
    if (after != null) args.addAll(listOf("-A", after))
    vcs.commandExecutor.execute(args)
}
```

## Commands
- `jj new` - Create new change after @
- `jj new -m "desc"` - Create with description
- `jj new -A <change-id>` - Create after specific change

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dialog for new change options
- [ ] Optional description field
- [ ] Optional "after" change selection
- [ ] Success notification
- [ ] Refreshes changes view
- [ ] Updates current change display

## References
See ROADMAP.md Phase 2 - Simple Write Operations
